name: HMS Ulva (T 248)
events: 8 Apr 1943 HMS Taku (Lt. A.J.W. Pitt, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Plymouth with HMS Pearl (T/Lt. G.K. Hill, RNVR) and HMS Ulva (Skr. E.R. Harris, RNR) and later with HMS Ambrose Pare (Skr. J.W. Morris, RNR) and HMS Lincolnshire (A/Skr.Lt. S.L. Larner, RNR). Also a practice attack was made on HMS Lincolnshire. (1)
25 Apr 1943 HMS Taku (Lt. A.J.W. Pitt, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Plymouth.

First with HMS Ulva (Skr. E.R. Harris, RNR) and HMS Cornelian (T/A/Lt.Cdr. S. Gorrell, RNR) and then with ORP Krakowiak (Kpt.mar. (Lt.Cdr.) J.A. Tchorznicki) and HMS Goathland (Lt.Cdr. E.N. Pumphrey, RN, DSO and 2 Bars, DSC, RN). (1)
4 May 1945 Around 1615 A/4, HMS Unrivalled (Lt. R.P. Fitzgerald, DSC, RN) returned to Plymouth from exercises.

She was escorted back to Plymouth by HMS Ulva (T/A/Skr.Lt. A.N. Mace, RNR) which had joined around 0820 A/4 having departed Plymouth around 0200 A/4 for the rendezvous position in 49°12'N, 04°20'W at 0900 A/4. (2)
9 May 1945 Around 0030 B/9, HMS Unrivalled (Lt. R.P. Fitzgerald, DSC, RN) returned to Plymouth after the exercises had been cancelled the previous day.

She was escorted back to Plymouth by HMS Ulva (T/A/Skr.Lt. A.N. Mace, RNR) which had joined around 1845 A/9 having departed Plymouth earlier in the day for the rendezvous position in 49°12'N, 04°20'W at 1900 A/9. (3)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
